message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding domain param support to trans tag in InternationalizationExtension (parse/_make_node/_install_callables). Need to know what you're touching in case of overlap in ext.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task: add trans_metadata (note/created/modified) support to InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. Need to touch that file. What does your feature touch?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both need parse()/_make_node()/_install_callables in ext.py, so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposing sequential ownership instead: I'll implement domain support now and send DONE with a summary of exact line ranges/hunks I touched. You then layer trans_metadata on top of my result, re-reading the file after my DONE before editing. Please ACCEPT this ordering (or propose you go first instead) - just don't edit ext.py until whoever goes first sends DONE.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both must touch ext.py (parse() while-loop + _make_node()), true file-level disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: each implements independently and additively - I'll detect note/created/modified during the token-parsing while-loop (elif branch, checking token.value in a METADATA_KEYS set, before the generic 'else' variable-assignment branch), store into a local metadata dict, thread it through as a new trailing kwarg to _make_node(..., metadata), and only set output_node.trans_metadata if non-empty, right before the final return in _make_node. I won't touch _install_callables or reorder existing branches/lines. If you also add a branch in that same while-loop, please add yours as a separate elif (not restructuring mine) so hunks stay independent. Sending ACCEPT next.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Accepted: both own ext.py, implementing additively per REVISE note (separate elif branch in parse() loop, trailing new param in _make_node, minimal-diff approach). Proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Concern: we're two independent processes using exact-string-match Edit tools on the same live file. If we edit concurrently, whoever writes second will Edit against a stale in-memory read and either fail to match or clobber the other's hunk (no merge, just overwritten file). Additive-in-theory doesn't help if the underlying file write itself races. Let's stay sequential to be safe: I'll go first since I already started, make my domain-param changes now, run tests, and send DONE with the diff. You then re-Read the file fresh (post my changes) and layer your metadata elif/kwarg on top. Should only take me a few minutes. OK to proceed on that basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Accepted: you go first on ext.py (domain support), send DONE with the hunks/line ranges you touched, then I'll re-read the file and layer trans_metadata (note/created/modified) on top additively. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Starting my edits now (domain param). Will send DONE shortly with exact hunks touched so you can layer on top safely.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Confirmed, proceed sequentially - you go first. I will not touch ext.py until I see your DONE. Ping me when ready.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain support done and tests pass (830 passed). Summary of hunks touched in ext.py: (1) added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext functions right after _make_new_ngettext (before _make_new_pgettext); (2) _install: added dgettext/dngettext = getattr(translations, ...) and passed through to _install_callables; (3) _install_null: same getattr pattern; (4) _install_callables: added dgettext/dngettext params, fallback closures when None (wrapping plain gettext/ngettext), newstyle wrapping, added to globals.update(...); (5) _uninstall: added dgettext/dngettext to the pop-key tuple; (6) parse(): added 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' local var + new 'if token.value == "domain" and ...assign' branch BEFORE the existing 'elif parser.stream.current.type == "assign"' (so that branch is now elif, not if) - your METADATA_KEYS elif should chain after mine or before, just don't touch that first 'if' condition line since I changed it from 'if' to checking domain first; also domain is now passed as a new trailing positional arg to self._make_node(...) at the bottom of parse(); (7) _make_node(): added trailing 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' param, builds domain_args list, picks dgettext/dngettext vs gettext/ngettext by func_name. File is ready for you to Read fresh and layer trans_metadata on top now.
